scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: 8 read streams (features, products, ideas, releases, initiatives, goals, epics, users). Aha!'s real API v1 surface is very large (strategic models, custom fields, custom layouts, custom reports, comments, to-dos, requirements, teams, integrations, webhooks, knowledge-base pages, workflows, competitors, personas, creative briefs, capacity investments, account backups, and more); this pass covers every core planning/roadmap object list endpoint reachable as a flat, un-nested `GET /api/v1/<resource>` list under this dialect's existing page_number pagination and bearer auth, matching the shape all 6 pre-existing streams already share. Writes remain unimplemented (`capabilities.write: false`) — see the ENGINE_GAP recorded in docs.md: every Aha! mutation endpoint requires a resource-named JSON envelope wrapper (e.g. `{"feature": {...}}`, `{"idea": {...}}`) around the body, which this dialect's write-body construction (the record's own fields, minus path_fields, sent as the top-level JSON body) has no mechanism to express; declaring a write action here would silently send a wrong-shaped, envelope-less body that the real Aha! API would reject or misinterpret, which the conventions.md §5 meta-rule forbids shipping as a workaround.

excluded.reason: single-feature detail; identical record shape to the features list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
ENGINE_GAP: feature creation requires a `{"feature": {...}}` JSON envelope wrapper this dialect's flat write-body construction cannot express; see docs.md
ENGINE_GAP: feature update requires a `{"feature": {...}}` JSON envelope wrapper this dialect's flat write-body construction cannot express; see docs.md
no allow-listed reverse-ETL delete writes; feature deletion is irreversible and out of scope
comments are enumerable only per already-covered parent feature (or idea/initiative/etc.), requiring a fan_out hop not modeled this pass; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
ENGINE_GAP: comment creation requires a `{"comment": {...}}` JSON envelope wrapper this dialect cannot express

to-dos are enumerable only per already-covered parent feature (or release/product/epic/idea), requiring a fan_out hop not modeled this pass; the flat top-level to-dos list endpoint's own real path could not be confirmed with sufficient confidence from available docs this pass
single-product detail; identical record shape to the products list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
ENGINE_GAP: product creation requires a `{"product": {...}}` JSON envelope wrapper this dialect cannot express
ENGINE_GAP: product update requires a `{"product": {...}}` JSON envelope wrapper this dialect cannot express
competitors are enumerable only per already-covered parent product (`/products/{id}/competitors`), not a flat top-level list; would require a fan_out hop not modeled this pass
teams are enumerable only per already-covered parent product, not a flat top-level list; would require a fan_out hop not modeled this pass
single-idea detail; identical record shape to the ideas list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

ENGINE_GAP: idea update requires a `{"idea": {...}}` JSON envelope wrapper this dialect cannot express
no allow-listed reverse-ETL delete writes; idea deletion is irreversible and out of scope
promotes an idea to a feature/epic/initiative; a workflow state-transition action layered on the same envelope-wrapper ENGINE_GAP as other idea mutations
comments are enumerable only per already-covered parent idea, requiring a fan_out hop not modeled this pass
votes are enumerable only per already-covered parent idea, requiring a fan_out hop not modeled this pass

no allow-listed reverse-ETL delete writes; release deletion is irreversible and out of scope

strategic-planning canvas objects (visions/positionings) are a distinct planning-tool surface from the core roadmap objects this connector covers; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
custom-field schema/definition metadata, not a syncable business-object stream
configured third-party integration connections/credentials metadata, not a syncable business-object stream
workflow-status definition metadata (the set of statuses a product's records can be in), not itself a syncable business-object stream

authenticated-user profile introspection, not a syncable object stream
